scope: Captain Ether starter items, including Batch 001 core radio procedure, Batch 002 marina/harbour basics, Batch 003 navigation reports, Batch 004 safety warnings, Batch 005 urgency basics, Batch 007 traffic/collision basics, Batch 008 VTS/port-control basics, and Batch 009 onboard operations basics after merge.
purpose: Regression-oriented Sea Speak accept/reject pairs. These pairs protect against rejecting clear maritime meaning and against accepting changed maritime meaning.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Opposite vessel sides must never be accepted as typo-level variants.
Stern is a vessel part; astern is a relative position behind the vessel.
Over invites a reply; out closes the exchange. Batch 001: Over invites a reply; out closes or suspends the exchange. Wait out must not become out alone.
Roger acknowledges receipt; affirmative means yes/confirmed.
Adjacent channel numbers must not pass through fuzzy matching.
Heading reports train the three-digit radio form; bare 90 is not equivalent for this item.

phrase_eta_001: eta one five zero zero utc

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: ETA time digits must not be corrected by typo or semantic matching.
Repeat is not accepted as a substitute for say again in this Sea Speak training.
Roger acknowledges receipt, affirmative means yes, and correct confirms accuracy.
Answer value is operationally opposite and must not be fuzzed.
Read back is receiver confirmation; say again asks the sender to retransmit.
Channel digits must not be typo-corrected or semantically substituted.
ETA time digits must not be corrected by typo or semantic matching.

phrase_core_i_spell_aurora_001: i spell aurora alfa uniform romeo oscar romeo alfa
i spell aurora alpha uniform romeo oscar romeo alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Confirmed: keep official ICAO/IMO spelling alphabet form Alfa; Alpha stays wrong for this training item.
Advice is the message-marker noun; advise is a verb. The typo layer must not accept this one-letter grammatical change.
Berth is the harbour place; birth is an ordinary English word and must not pass through typo matching.
Confirmed strict for Batch 002: berth stays separate from dock, quay, pier, and slip.
Mooring, berthing, and anchoring are related but not interchangeable in this batch.
Line is the trained mooring term; rope remains wrong for these drills.
Finder is a one-letter ordinary word and bumper is marina slang; both must stay wrong for the trained Sea Speak fender term.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: port side to / starboard side to
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port and starboard side-to instructions are operationally opposite and must not be fuzzed or translated to left/right.
Relative positions and alongside mooring language must stay distinct.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: stand by outside / wait out / do not answer

phrase_marina_stand_by_outside_001: do not answer outside the marina

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Stand by outside is a harbour waiting instruction, not wait out or do not answer.

Harbour movement verbs have different operational meanings; go ahead is a radio permission phrase, not a movement synonym here. Compact B12 is also rejected until alphanumeric berth formats receive matcher QA.
The draft trains formal request wording and rejects informal need phrasing.
Routine marina services must not substitute for each other.
Arrival and departure are opposite workflow states.
Heading, course, and bearing are related navigation terms but not interchangeable.

phrase_nav_position_east_reporting_point: position east of reporting point alpha
destination east of reporting point alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Current position, destination, waypoint, and reporting point carry different navigation meanings.
ETA digits must not fuzz-match or be semantically substituted.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: 1400 UTC / 1400Z / one four zero zero UTC

expr_nav_eta_1400_utc_001: ETA one four zero zero UTC
phrase_nav_eta_1400_001: My ETA is one four zero zero UTC
phrase_nav_read_back_eta_position_001: read back ETA 1400 UTC and position
read back ETA 1400 local and position

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: UTC/Zulu/Z variants are item-local accepted forms; local time remains wrong.

phrase_nav_read_back_course_speed_001: read back course 090 and speed five knots
read back course 90 and speed five knots

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Three-digit radio heading/course/bearing form must stay protected; bare 090 is heading-local and must not validate course reports.
Speed units and distance units must not substitute for each other.

phrase_nav_distance_decimal_one_five_001: distance is one decimal five nautical miles to the reporting point
phrase_nav_position_west_point_distance_: position is one decimal five nautical miles west of reporting point bravo

phrase_nav_distance_decimal_one_five_001: distance is one point five nautical miles to the reporting point
distance is one dot five nautical miles to the reporting point
phrase_nav_position_west_point_distance_: position is one point five nautical miles west of reporting point bravo

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Batch 001 strictness keeps decimal as the trained radio form.

phrase_nav_position_north_marina_001: position is two nautical miles north of the marina
phrase_nav_position_south_breakwater_001: position is one cable south of the breakwater
phrase_nav_position_east_reporting_point: position east of reporting point alpha

phrase_nav_position_north_marina_001: position is two nautical miles south of the marina
phrase_nav_position_south_breakwater_001: position is one cable north of the breakwater
phrase_nav_position_east_reporting_point: position west of reporting point alpha
phrase_nav_position_west_point_distance_: position is one decimal five nautical miles east of reporting point bravo

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Cardinal directions are operationally different and must not fuzz-match.

phrase_nav_position_port_side_channel_00: position port side of the channel
phrase_nav_position_starboard_side_chann: position starboard side of the channel

phrase_nav_position_port_side_channel_00: position starboard side of the channel
position left side of the channel
phrase_nav_position_starboard_side_chann: position port side of the channel
position right side of the channel

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port/starboard remain protected inside navigation position reports.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: say again position / read back position
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Read back confirms received information; say again asks for retransmission.

phrase_safety_securite_nav_obstruction_a: security security security navigation warning obstruction two cables north of reporting point alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Securite/Sécurité is the safety signal; ordinary English security must stay wrong.

phrase_safety_securite_three_times_001: pan pan pan pan pan pan

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Safety signal must not collapse into other radio signal families.
Batch 004 is safety information only.
Message category words carry different operational meaning.
Advice is a noun; advise is a verb-form lookalike and must not pass typo matching.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: restricted visibility / poor visibility / reduced visibility / visibility good

phrase_safety_restricted_visibility_mari: restricted visibility in the marina approach
poor visibility in the marina approach
reduced visibility in the marina approach
visibility good in the marina approach
phrase_safety_restricted_visibility_chan: navigation warning poor visibility in the approach channel until 1400 utc
navigation warning reduced visibility in the approach channel until 1400 utc

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Restricted visibility is the trained term and must not accept ordinary paraphrases or opposite visibility state.
Obstacle is ordinary English but Batch 004 trains obstruction as the safety-warning report term.

phrase_safety_navigation_warning_obstruc: weather warning obstruction in the channel

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Navigation and weather warnings are separate safety-information categories.
Hazard, obstruction, and ordinary danger wording are not interchangeable in this draft.

phrase_safety_weather_warning_wind_sea_0: securite weather warning visibility from north sea state rough

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Weather, wind, sea state, and visibility are related but distinct report elements.

phrase_safety_read_back_warning_channel_: read back safety warning channel 16 1400 utc
say again safety warning channel one six one four zero zero utc

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Read back confirms received information; say again asks for retransmission.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: exact positions, channels, times, bearings, distances, directions

phrase_safety_hazard_two_cables_north_00: hazard 2 cables north of the marina
hazard two cables south of the marina
hazard two nautical miles north of the marina
phrase_safety_hazard_bearing_distance_00: hazard bearing zero nine zero distance two nautical miles

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Safety-warning numerics and directions must not be fuzzed or loosely substituted.
Urgency, safety, and distress signals must not collapse into one radio signal family.
Operational message category changes the meaning of the call.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: engine failure / steering failure / power failure
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Failure type changes vessel capability and the assistance needed.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: disabled vessel / not under command / restricted manoeuvrability

phrase_urgency_disabled_vessel_001: vessel restricted in ability to manoeuvre

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Basic urgency disabled-vessel wording should not blur into distress or legal-status categories.

phrase_urgency_medical_assistance_requir: medical assistance required for one person
medical advice required for one person
medical assistance required for two persons

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Medical assistance, advice, evacuation, and person counts must stay item-specific.
Towage support must not imply rescue, SAR, tug operations, or pilot service.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: stand by / keep listening watch / go ahead
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Procedure words carry different radio actions and should remain strict.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: exact channels, times, positions, directions, distances, and counts

phrase_urgency_position_north_marina_001: position two nautical miles north of the marina
phrase_urgency_medical_assistance_requir: medical assistance required for one person

phrase_urgency_position_north_marina_001: position two nautical miles south of the marina
position two cables north of the marina
phrase_urgency_medical_assistance_requir: medical assistance required for two persons

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Urgency details are operationally significant and must not be fuzzed or loosely substituted.

phrase_traffic_alter_starboard_001: I will alter course to starboard.
phrase_traffic_alter_port_001: I will alter course to port.
phrase_traffic_intend_pass_port_001: I intend to pass on your port side.
phrase_traffic_intend_pass_starboard_001: I intend to pass on your starboard side.
phrase_traffic_crossing_from_starboard_0: You are crossing from my starboard side.
phrase_traffic_crossing_from_port_001: You are crossing from my port side.

phrase_traffic_crossing_from_starboard_0: you are crossing from my port side
phrase_traffic_crossing_from_port_001: you are crossing from my starboard side

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port/starboard side and alteration cues must not collapse into ordinary right/left or opposite side.

phrase_traffic_passing_astern_001: I will pass astern of you.
phrase_traffic_passing_ahead_001: I will pass ahead of you.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Ahead and astern reverse the planned passing relation.

phrase_traffic_passing_astern_001: I will pass astern of you.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Stern is a vessel part; astern is a relative position behind the vessel.

phrase_traffic_alter_starboard_001: I will alter course to starboard.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Course alteration must not become radio channel switching.
Speed reduction must not become stop-engine or speed-report wording.
Collision-risk language must stay separate from an actual collision event.
Closest point, time to closest point, ETA, and units must remain item-specific.
Give-way and stand-on roles carry opposite collision-avoidance duties.

phrase_traffic_crossing_from_starboard_0: You are crossing from my starboard side.
phrase_traffic_crossing_from_port_001: You are crossing from my port side.

phrase_traffic_crossing_from_starboard_0: you are crossing from my port side
phrase_traffic_crossing_from_port_001: you are crossing from my starboard side
phrase_traffic_i_am_overtaking_001: i will pass astern of you
i am passing ahead of you

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Traffic situation labels must not collapse across crossing, overtaking, and passing intentions.
Bearing observations must stay separate from heading and course wording.

phrase_vts_report_passing_alpha_001: Report when passing reporting point Alpha.
report when passing reporting point bravo

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Request asks for authority or service; report provides information.
Instruction, advice, and information have different authority weight.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station

phrase_vts_call_aurora_001: VTS, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
marina control this is sailing yacht aurora
pilot station this is sailing yacht aurora

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Station identity must not collapse across traffic authority, marina, and pilot stations.
Pilotage and tug assistance are separate services.
Entry and departure instructions are opposite port movements.
Permission and refusal are opposite authority results.

phrase_vts_at_reporting_point_alpha_001: I am at reporting point Alpha.
i am at reporting point bravo
i am passing reporting point alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port areas and reporting points are operationally different locations.
Channel numbers must remain exact.
Proceed, hold, and wait are different movement instructions.

phrase_vts_outbound_vessel_fairway_001: Traffic information: outbound vessel in fairway.
traffic information inbound vessel in fairway
traffic information outbound vessel at anchorage
traffic information pilot vessel in fairway

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Inbound and outbound traffic directions must not be interchangeable.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: hand over watch / take over watch

phrase_onboard_take_over_bridge_watch_00: I take over the bridge watch.
i hand over the bridge watch

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Watch handover and takeover are opposite watch-transfer actions.
Order completion, action completion, and ongoing helm order language must stay separate.
Port and starboard helm orders reverse the vessel steering order.
Anchor handling, mooring lines, and berth concepts must not collapse.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: let go anchor / heave up anchor
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Let go anchor and heave up/weigh anchor are opposite anchor operations.
Securing and releasing lines are opposite mooring actions.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: bow station / stern station / port station / starboard station
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Mooring station positions must stay exact.
Stand by as a command and standing by as a readiness status must stay separate.
Safety checks and emergency actions are different workflows.
Onboard emergency type changes the required response.
Distress, urgency, and safety signals must remain strict.
Mayday distress language must not weaken Pan-Pan or Securite boundaries.
Relay means forwarding another station's distress and must not collapse into own distress or urgency relay.

phrase_distress_my_position_4550n_01310e: My position is four five five zero north, zero one three one zero east.
My course is four five five zero north
My destination is zero one three one zero east
Position four five north zero one east

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Distress location information must stay distinct from course, bearing, and destination data.
Distress identity fields are separate and should not be interchangeable.
Nature-of-distress casualties must remain exact.

phrase_distress_tow_not_enough_001: Towage is not enough, rescue required.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Rescue-level distress help must stay above port-service and towage language.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: persons on board / persons overboard / abandoning vessel
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: People-count, overboard, and abandoning-vessel concepts must not merge.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: read back / relay / say again / cancel

phrase_distress_read_back_position_natur: Read back position and nature of distress.
Relay position and nature of distress

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Distress workflow verbs have different operational meanings.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: distress traffic / safety watch / routine traffic

phrase_distress_all_stations_keep_clear_: All stations, keep clear for distress traffic.

phrase_distress_silence_distress_traffic: Keep listening watch for safety traffic
phrase_distress_all_stations_keep_clear_: All stations keep listening watch for safety
All stations switch to marina channel

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Distress channel protection is not Securite listening watch or normal traffic.
Over expects a reply; out closes the communication.
Received and yes are different radio meanings.
Say again is a repetition request; read back is confirmation.
Port and starboard are opposite vessel sides.
Ahead and astern are opposite relative directions.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: channel one six / channel one three
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Channel digits must remain exact.
Three-digit heading and bearing forms must preserve leading zero.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: one four zero zero / one five zero zero
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Spoken time digits must preserve exact value.
Radio signal families must not collapse.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: alter course to port / alter course to starboard
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Side and action changes alter the command.
Give way and stand on are opposite traffic obligations.

matcher_suggestions: Use this file as a regression seed: every should_accept answer should be correct and every should_reject answer should be wrong.
Keep dangerous minimal pairs in explicit tests whenever alias or typo logic changes.
Do not add global aliases that collapse Sea Speak distinctions, especially port/starboard, stern/astern, over/out, roger/affirmative, channel digits, heading digits, or ETA digits.
The typo layer should reject tokens containing digits when the expected token also contains digits but is not identical, for example 1500Z versus 1400Z.
The safety signal Securite/Sécurité should not accept the ordinary English word security as a spelling variant.
Marker nouns must not accept verb-form lookalikes through typo matching, for example advice versus advise.
Harbour lookalikes must not pass through typo matching: berth versus birth, fender versus finder, and fenders versus finders.
Keep compact berth identifiers such as B12 rejected until alphanumeric berth matching is intentionally designed and regression-tested.
Navigation reports must keep heading/course/bearing, ETA values, radio numeric forms, units, decimal wording, cardinal directions, port/starboard, and say again/read back boundaries protected.
Traffic/collision matching must preserve port/starboard, ahead/astern, CPA/TCPA/ETA, give-way/stand-on roles, crossing/overtaking/passing, and bearing/heading/course distinctions.

VTS/port-control matching must preserve station identity, instruction/advice/information, request/report, pilot/tug/tow, entry/departure, permission negation, exact channels, port-area terms, movement instructions, and inbound/outbound distinctions.

Onboard-operations matching must preserve watch handover/takeover, helm order direction, anchor and line actions, station positions, stand-by status, safety-check versus emergency-action wording, and fire/flooding/man-overboard distinctions.

resolved_matcher_findings.reason: Security is ordinary English and not the Sea Speak safety signal Securite/Sécurité.
1500Z changes the ETA from 1400Z; alphanumeric time tokens must not be fuzzy-matched.
Advice is the message-marker noun; advise is a verb-form lookalike and must not pass through typo matching.
Berth is a marina place; birth is an unrelated ordinary English word.
Request birth changes the trained berth-request meaning.
Fender is the trained mooring equipment; finder is an unrelated word.
Finders changes the trained fenders equipment term.
Finders changes the trained fenders equipment term.
